mechanism_narrative: Higher trailing 3-month growth in PAYEMS raises household income expectations and near-term earnings revisions, causing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS was 159724 as of the latest available snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if the PAYEMS 3-month growth coefficient is nonpositive in walk-forward tests or if top-quintile PAYEMS momentum produces 20-day SPX returns no higher than bottom-quintile momentum in more than 60% of rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS crosses above a restrictive threshold, equity discount rates remain elevated and valuation multiples compress, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS was 4.33 as of the latest available snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if triggered FEDFUNDS-above-4.25% observations have positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of cases and their mean return is not below the unconditional mean at p <= 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: A joint state of rising CPIAUCSL momentum and high FEDFUNDS signals persistent inflation pressure with limited easing capacity, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized change exceeds its 12-month change and FEDFUNDS is above 4.00%
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL was 321.500 and FEDFUNDS was 4.33 in the latest snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if the joint CPIAUCSL-FEDFUNDS trigger does not underperform the unconditional 60-day SPX return by at least 50 bps on average in walk-forward tests, or if the interaction term is not negative.

mechanism_narrative: A positive breakout in RSAFS indicates accelerating nominal consumption demand and stronger revenue expectations, causing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS was 715417.0 as of the latest available snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if RSAFS breakout signals have a nonpositive average 20-day SPX return or if the hit rate for positive returns after the trigger is below 50% over two consecutive rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A downward break in INDPRO signals weakening real activity and pressure on operating leverage, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO crosses below its 6-month moving average
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO was 103.5948 as of the latest available snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if INDPRO downside-cross triggers are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of observations or if the post-trigger mean return is statistically indistinguishable from baseline at p > 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: A rebound in HOUST reflects improving construction activity and cyclical credit demand, causing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST crosses above its 3-month moving average after being below it for at least 2 months
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST was 1256.0 as of the latest available snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST rebound triggers do not produce a positive average 60-day SPX return or if their average return is below the unconditional 60-day return in more than half of rolling 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in UNRATE signals deteriorating labor demand and recession-risk repricing, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE was 4.1 as of the latest available snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if the UNRATE 0.3 percentage point trigger is followed by positive 5-day SPX returns in more than 60% of cases or if the trigger coefficient is nonnegative in walk-forward logistic tests.

mechanism_narrative: Low UNRATE combined with decelerating CPIAUCSL inflation identifies a soft-landing state in which earnings risk stays contained while policy risk falls, causing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE is at or below 4.2% and CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized change is below its 12-month change
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE was 4.1 and CPIAUCSL was 321.500 in the latest snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if the joint low-UNRATE disinflation trigger has a nonpositive mean 20-day SPX return or if the interaction term between UNRATE and CPIAUCSL disinflation is not positive in rolling 3-year tests.
